title: Premium Dark-Mode Full-Stack Dashboard with Admin Panel

requirements.text: Apply a consistent premium dark-mode visual theme across the entire application: base background color #0B0E14, glassmorphism card/panel surfaces (semi-transparent with backdrop-blur and subtle border), indigo accent color for interactive elements, buttons, badges, and highlights. Use Space Grotesk and Inter fonts. Ensure all existing and new components inherit this theme.

quotes: premium dark-mode full-stack dashboard (#0B0E14) using Tailwind CSS with glassmorphism and indigo accents

acceptanceCriteria: Root background is #0B0E14
Cards and panels use glassmorphism style (bg-white/5 or similar, backdrop-blur, border border-white/10)
Indigo is the primary accent color for buttons, active states, and badges
Tailwind config reflects these tokens globally
No light-mode elements or conflicting color schemes are visible

requirements.text: Implement a responsive AppShell layout: on desktop (md+), render a fixed sidebar (SideNavigation) showing user profile avatar, display name, greeting (e.g., 'Good morning, [Name]'), role badge, and nav links. On mobile, hide the sidebar and show a persistent bottom navigation bar (BottomNavigation) with icon+label nav items. Both components must be role-aware and reflect authentication state.
acceptanceCriteria: Desktop sidebar is visible on screens md and above, hidden on smaller screens
Sidebar displays user avatar, display name, a time-based greeting, and role badge
Mobile bottom nav is visible only on small screens and is always fixed to the bottom
Navigation items are role-filtered (admin/owner items only shown to those roles)
Active route is visually highlighted in both sidebar and bottom nav

requirements.text: Build a Vertical Clip Preview component that displays the currently selected or most recently created clip in a 9:16 (mobile-ratio) aspect ratio container. It should show the clip thumbnail (if available), title, time range, duration, and viral score badge. This component should be prominently placed on the main dashboard/home view.
acceptanceCriteria: Preview container enforces a 9:16 aspect ratio
Clip title, start/end timestamps, and duration are displayed
Viral score badge is shown with color coding
Component renders a placeholder state when no clip is selected
Component is rendered on the main dashboard page

requirements.text: Enhance the AI caption editor to be a fully editable textarea with character count, markdown-friendly formatting hints, and a glassmorphism card wrapper. Ensure the YouTube upload scheduler page allows users to select a saved clip, pick a scheduled date/time, view and delete scheduled uploads, and shows a connected YouTube channel status badge. Both editors must be accessible from the main dashboard and/or dedicated pages.

quotes: Editors: Editable AI caption box and YouTube upload scheduler.

acceptanceCriteria: Caption editor textarea is editable with live character count
Caption editor card uses glassmorphism styling
Scheduler page lists existing scheduled uploads with delete option
Scheduler allows selecting a clip and a future datetime
YouTube channel connection status is visible on the scheduler page

requirements.text: Build or enhance the 'Trending in Niche' creator feed page (TrendingPage) to display a ranked leaderboard of trending clips with creator info, trend scores, and thumbnails in a social-feed style grid layout. Include a sidebar or section showing top trending analytics. Use glassmorphism cards with indigo accent highlights for top-ranked entries.
acceptanceCriteria: Trending page shows a ranked list/grid of clips with trend scores
Each clip card displays title, creator identifier, score, and rank badge
Top 3 entries are visually distinguished (e.g., gold/silver/bronze indigo accent)
Loading skeleton states are shown while data is fetching
Empty state is handled gracefully

requirements.text: Implement the Manager View (ContentManager page) as a full CRUD interface for content entries. The page must be accessible to users with 'manager', 'admin', or 'owner' roles. It should display a table/card list of content entries with inline or modal-based edit forms, add new entry form, and delete confirmation dialogs. Use glassmorphism card styling throughout.

quotes: Manager View: Simple CRUD UI for manual content updates.

acceptanceCriteria: Content entries are listed in a table or card grid
Each entry has Edit and Delete action buttons
A form or modal is available to add new content entries
Delete action shows a confirmation dialog before proceeding
Page is gated to manager/admin/owner roles; other users see an access denied message
All mutations use existing useContentEntries hooks

requirements.text: Add backend support for system control commands: a 'server restart' action and a 'server shutdown' action callable only by the owner principal. These should log the action to the activity log with timestamp and caller principal. The restart action resets relevant mutable state counters (e.g., messageCounter) while preserving core data. The shutdown action records the event. Both return a confirmation text result.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend exposes restartServer() and shutdownServer() public shared functions
Both functions are owner-only; unauthorized callers receive an error
Each call appends an entry to the activityLogs stable map with action name, caller, and timestamp
restartServer resets mutable counters/state as appropriate without erasing clip/user data
Functions return a descriptive confirmation string

requirements.text: Build the Admin Panel (AdminPanel component and supporting components) as a gated dashboard section visible only to admin and owner roles. It must include: (1) User & Clip Management — a searchable user table with Ban/Unban toggles and a batch clip deletion tool that selects multiple clips by user ID and deletes them in bulk. (2) System Controls — buttons to trigger restartServer and shutdownServer backend calls with confirmation dialogs. (3) Analytics — real-time activity log table (polling), a click-tracking counter display, and a 'Videos Per Hour' bar chart using Recharts. (4) Support Messaging — a form to send a direct message to a user by their User ID.

quotes: User/Clip Management: Ban/Unban tools and batch deletion.
Analytics: Real-time activity logs, click tracking, and 'Videos Per Hour' charts.

acceptanceCriteria: Admin panel route/page is only accessible to admin and owner roles
User management table lists all users with current status and role, with Ban/Unban toggle buttons
Batch deletion UI allows selecting multiple clips associated with a user and deleting them
System Controls section shows Restart and Shutdown buttons, each with a confirmation dialog before calling the backend
Activity log table auto-refreshes every 15 seconds and is searchable
A 'Videos Per Hour' bar chart displays clip creation counts grouped by hour using Recharts
Click tracking stat (total clip views/interactions) is displayed as a counter card
Support messaging form accepts a User ID and message text and sends via useSendAdminMessage hook

requirements.text: Add backend support for batch clip deletion by admin: a function deleteClipsByUser(userId: Text) that deletes all clips belonging to a specific user, callable only by admin or owner principals. Also add a getClickStats() function that returns the total number of clip interactions/downloads logged, and a getVideosPerHour() function that returns an array of {hour: Int, count: Nat} records for the last 24 hours based on activityLogs.
acceptanceCriteria: deleteClipsByUser(userId) removes all clips in the clips map with matching userId and is admin/owner gated
getClickStats() returns a Nat count of download/post activity log entries
getVideosPerHour() returns an array of hour-bucket records covering the last 24 hours with clip creation counts
All three functions are exposed as public shared query or update functions as appropriate
Unauthorized callers receive an error result

constraints: All Motoko logic must remain in the single backend/main.mo actor file
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Use existing hooks (useContentEntries, useSendAdminMessage, useActivityLogs, useSetUserStatus, useClips, etc.) rather than duplicating logic
Admin panel and system controls must be gated behind owner/admin role checks on both frontend and backend
Background base color must be #0B0E14 throughout
nonGoals: Real-time WebSocket or push notification features
Third-party social media API integrations (Twitter, Instagram, etc.)
Actual server process termination on shutdown — only log the event in backend
